api_alias: deepseek-v4-flash (verified live against api.deepseek.com/models and a chat call)
immutable_model_id: open-weights reference: huggingface.co/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash @ revision sha 60d8d70770c6776ff598c94bb586a859a38244f1 (repo created 2026-04-22; MIT license; safetensors)
release_time: 2026-04-24 (official DeepSeek API news post news260424; HF repo created 2026-04-22)
claimed_knowledge_cutoff: NOT explicitly documented. Conservative upper bound used instead: training data necessarily predates the public release (2026-04-24).
weight_hashes: addressable via the HF revision sha above (per-file LFS sha256 addressable at that revision); NOT re-hashed locally (284B params — no local inference capability)
tokenizer_hash: addressable at the same HF revision
mutable_serving_risk: PRESENT: the hosted alias is not proven byte-identical to the open checkpoint, and the provider may silently update serving weights (HF repo lastModified 2026-06-22 shows post-release repo activity). This is why the tier is B, not A.
exact_reason: Tier A (immutable local checkpoint) requires inference on the pinned 284B open checkpoint; no GPU inference capability exists in this environment, so serving-weight identity cannot be enforced. Tier B is defensible because: (1) an exact API alias exists and is verified live; (2) the release date is documented by two official sources; (3) EVERY benchmark question is REQUIRED to have opened AFTER 2026-04-24, so even the strongest form of weight mutation cannot inject knowledge of outcomes that had not occurred at release — outcome leakage would additionally require post-release retraining on post-release events, which the leakage probes measure per row; (4) name-only/no-evidence probes remain mandatory on every row.

eligible_event_window.ordering_rule: model release S (2026-04-24) < question open Q <= forecast cutoff T < resolution R, proven per row from the market archive's server timestamps

tier_a_unblock: pin + host HF revision 60d8d707 on rented GPU infrastructure with hash-verified weights; re-run the same frozen benchmark
